ocr.text: In the table, the analysis focuses on liver, skeletal muscle, faeces, and their respective cholesterol and TAG metrics under three
dietary conditions: Baked salmon diet, Raw salmon diet, and Control diet, along with their standard deviations and ANOVA
results. For liver cholesterol (μmol/g), the Baked salmon diet shows a mean of 7.1 with a standard deviation of 2.3, the Raw
salmon diet presents a mean of 8.1 and a standard deviation of 1.1, while the Control diet has a mean of 9.3 with a standard
deviation of 2.1, resulting in a P value of 0.20. The TAG (μmol/g) for liver under the Baked salmon diet is 125.3 (SD 30.3), the
Raw salmon diet is 117.7 (SD 39.2), and the Control diet is 147.1 (SD 43.2) with a P value of 0.44.
Baked salmon diet  Raw salmon diet  Control diet  ANOVA
Mean  SD  Mean  SD  Mean  SD  P
Liver
Skeletal muscle
Faeces
In skeletal muscle, cholesterol (μmol/g) for the Baked salmon diet is 1.6 (SD 0.1), the Raw salmon diet is 1.7 (SD 0.2), and the
Control diet is 1.6 (SD 0.2), yielding a P value of 0.78. TAG (μmol/g) in skeletal muscle shows 17.4 (SD 4.5) for the Baked
salmon diet, 26.4 (SD 15.2) for the Raw salmon diet, and 15.7 (SD 5.3) for the Control diet with a P value of 0.19. In faeces,
cholesterol (μmol/24 h) for the Baked salmon diet is 21.0 (SD 4.2), the Raw salmon diet is 23.0 (SD 6.8), and the Control diet is
18.0 (SD 5.6), with a P value of 0.37. TAG (μmol/24 h) for faeces under the Baked salmon diet is 2.6 (SD 1.3), the Raw salmon
diet is 3.3 (SD 2.5), and the Control diet is 2.3 (SD 0.8), resulting in a P value of 0.61. Lastly, bile acids (μmol/24 h) for the
